commit.message:
fix: remove lexical from peerDependencies — bundled as dependency

lexical is an internal implementation detail. Users never touch the
Lexical API directly (no public types, no exported Lexical instances).
Making it a peer was wrong — it required users to install an extra
package they never use.

- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/core
- Remove peerDependencies.lexical from @lexify/plugins
- Update install instructions in all READMEs and docs

Users now only need:
  pnpm add @lexify/core

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Scaffold monorepo: Next.js + NestJS + Drizzle + Docker Compose

Monorepo structure (Turborepo):
- apps/web: Next.js 14 App Router + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui
- apps/api: NestJS + Fastify, health checks, OpenAPI/Swagger
- packages/shared-types: ReadinessStatus, Organization, Personnel types
- packages/database: Drizzle ORM with full 14-table PostgreSQL schema

Infrastructure:
- docker-compose.yml: PostgreSQL 16, Redis 7, Keycloak 24
- ESLint + Prettier root configs
- All 4 packages build successfully via turbo

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

progress. Fixes the issue where players couldn't retry after completing.

commit.message:
simplify starting the dev server from scratch (#5433)

* feat: add prestart/prestartSSL hooks to auto-build prerequisites

Automatically runs buildDLL:dev and buildModules before starting the
dev server if common-dist/common-manifest.json or package build outputs
are missing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: move prestart build check into shared script

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: check every buildable package for dist/index.js in prestart script

Previously used a glob that only checked if any dist/index.js existed,
not all of them. Now iterates packages with a build script to ensure
every one has its artifact before skipping buildModules.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: use next available port if 3000 is already in use

Uses webpack-dev-server's built-in 'auto' port selection so yarn start
doesn't fail with EADDRINUSE when port 3000 is taken. Can still be
overridden with PORT=<n> yarn start.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* revert: remove auto port selection change

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: add set -euo pipefail to prestart script for fail-fast behavior

Ensures a failed buildDLL:dev or buildModules stops the process
immediately rather than continuing and hitting the same missing
artifact error downstream.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add progress indicator and stop button for in-flight requests (#155)

* feat: add progress indicator and stop button for in-flight requests

Add processing phase state machine (idle → thinking → generating) to
replace binary _isProcessing flag. Show animated "thinking..." indicator
during request setup and "generating..." during streaming. Add stop
button that cancels in-flight requests via session.abort().

Closes #122

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add processing phase and stop button tests

Add 7 tests covering the processing phase state machine:
- thinking/generating/idle phase transitions
- idle-on-error recovery
- full lifecycle sequence
- stopRequest triggering session.abort()
- stopRequest no-op when idle

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* squad: progress indicator + stop button orchestration logs, session summary, and decision merge

Scribe merge:
- Orchestration logs for MacReady (architecture), Blair (implementation), Windows (testing)
- Session log for progress indicator + stop button work
- Merged 3 decision inbox files into decisions.md (no duplicates; consolidated into single entry)
- Deleted inbox files after merge

Issue resolved: #122 (Stop Button Robustness)

Work summary:
- 4-state processing phase state machine (idle → thinking → generating → idle)
- Progress indicator (animated dots) with dynamic text
- Stop button with session.abort() cancellation
- 7 new tests, all 301 tests passing
- Zero regressions

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Move attach buttons (Selection, File) to left side with workspace
indicator on the right. Replace separate stop button with send/stop
toggle — Send button becomes Stop during processing.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address PR review — stop/cancel lifecycle and race conditions

- Add request-scoped cancellation flag for thinking phase stop
- Track request ID to prevent finally-block clobbering
- Properly await session.abort() with error logging
- Add safety timeout for stuck stop button state
- Block duplicate sends during thinking phase

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: add cancel lifecycle and race condition tests

Tests for thinking-phase cancellation, request ID clobbering
prevention, abort error handling, and duplicate send prevention.

* docs: update windows history with cancel lifecycle testing learnings

commit.message:
feat: Phase 18 — Offline Smart Mode with local LLM

Add local LLM support via llama-server subprocess (SmolLM2-360M):
- llm.go: Managed subprocess lifecycle (start/stop/health-check)
- llm_download.go: Download llama-server + GGUF model from releases
- Provider selection: OpenAI / Local / Auto with fallback chain
- Keyword-based template matching against active app (EXE + title)
- Default keywords for all 12 builtin presets (email, meeting, etc.)
- GetActiveWindowTitle() via Win32 GetWindowTextW
- Template metadata: description + keywords for auto-detection
- Configurable fallback preset (default: cleanup)
- Premium UI: provider segmented control, LLM download card,
  fallback preset dropdown, keyword chip input for custom templates
- Full EN + DE localization (28 new translation keys)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

- Guard JSON.parse in rowToWebhook with try/catch, default to []
- Guard JSON.parse in rowToSavedSearch with try/catch, default to null
- Guard JSON.parse in loadDbConnectorConfig with try/catch, throw ConfigError
- Push dateFrom/dateTo filters into SQL WHERE in listDocuments (before LIMIT)
- Validate negative limit in resolveSelector, throw ValidationError
- Replace manual substring extension parsing with path.extname() in packs.ts
- Verified reporter.ts is already tracked on development (no action needed)
- Added tests for all fixes (corrupted JSON, SQL-level date filtering, negative limit)

Closes #342

commit.message:
Add ambiguity prevention rules and expand word substitutions for grade 12→8 push (#8)

* Initial plan

* Add ambiguity prevention rules and expanded word substitutions to push grade 12→8

Based on real-world research (Arkansas AG Opinion 2025-046, NY S1381A):
- Add AMBIGUITY PREVENTION section (8 rules) to system prompt
- Add AMBIGUITY CHECK to self-check section
- Add anti-ambiguity rules to refinement and targeted refinement prompts
- Add 10 new phrase substitutions (e.g., 'for the purpose of' → 'to')
- Add 13 new word substitutions (e.g., 'expenditure' → 'spending')
- Add 20+ new word/phrase substitution guidance to system prompt
- Add 24 new tests covering all new functionality
- All 121 tests pass

* Address code review: fix inasmuch→inasmuch as, acquisition→purchase, distribution→sharing

---------

Co-authored-by: copilot-swe-agent[bot] <198982749+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: YouGotCuboned <261487993+YouGotCuboned@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Arkansas has decided bills must be readable at an 8th grade level, while obviously our brightest are far above that. commit.message:
Complete Phase 2.3 + Phase 3: ADRs and UX design system

ADRs (4 files):
- 001-frontend-framework.md: Next.js 14 + shadcn/ui + Tailwind (Accepted)
- 002-backend-framework.md: NestJS with Fastify adapter (Accepted)
- 003-database.md: PostgreSQL 16 with closure table (Accepted)

Add 7 ZIVO design-system React components

- DocGenerator: 4-tab SSE streaming, copy-per-tab, JSZip download

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: restore Qt-first image capture order (v1.3.4 proven approach)

Reorder _try_capture_image() to match v1.3.4 behavior that worked for Win+Shift+S:
  1. Qt clipboard.image()  <- PRIMARY (v1.3.4 approach, proven reliable)
  2. PIL ImageGrab          <- fallback for CF_DIB/legacy GDI apps
  3. Win32 registered PNG   <- final fallback for PNG-only registered format sources

v1.3.4 worked because Qt correctly reads Win+Shift+S screenshots directly via
clipboard.image(). Placing Win32 reading first (v1.4.2) was unnecessary and
risked pre-empting Qt's reliable path. Win32 path kept as final fallback.

All 20 tests pass (test_clipboard_diag, test_monitor_integration, test_retry).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: add tool calling framework with Tavily web search

- Tool registry with abstract Tool base class, auto-registration, and
  user-defined tools via ~/.foundry-tui/tools.json
- Tavily Search as built-in web_search tool (free tier, 1K searches/mo)
- Full tool calling protocol in all 3 API clients (Azure OpenAI,
  Azure AI, Serverless) with streaming tool_calls delta accumulation
- Tool loop in chat flow: stream → detect tool_calls → execute →
  append results → re-call API (max 10 iterations)
- Collapsible ToolCallMessage UI widget showing tool name + args
- /tools command to list registered tools and model compatibility
- Status bar tool count indicator
- Setup scripts updated: --only-search flag, Tavily API key prompt
- Teardown scripts updated for TAVILY_API_KEY cleanup

Originally planned for Bing Search v7, but pivoted to Tavily after
discovering Bing Search was retired (Aug 2025) and Grounding with Bing
requires Azure AI Agent SDK (incompatible with chat completions API).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Initial plan

* Initial plan

* fix: stub indexedDB as undefined in useDraftAutosave tests to avoid hanging

The indexedDB.open mock never invoked onsuccess/onerror callbacks, causing
DraftStorage.init() Promise to never resolve. This led to test hangs and timeouts.

Solution: Stub indexedDB as undefined to exercise the localStorage fallback path,
avoiding the complexity of mocking IndexedDB's async callback behavior.

All 18 tests now pass successfully.

- Use event-specific SHAs (github.event.before/sha for push, pull_request.base/head.sha for PR)

commit.message:
Add background cache refresh to current glucose command

When fetching the current glucose reading, kick off a background
thread to refresh the last day of data in the local cache. This
ensures follow-up analytical queries (day, query, patterns, etc.)
have fresh data without an extra API round-trip.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: bulletproof Win+Shift+S image capture with retry + reorder + PIL hidden imports

clipboard_monitor.py:
- New _try_capture_image() helper tries Win32 PNG FIRST (before Qt opens clipboard)
  so Snipping Tool images are captured directly, not via CF_DIB synthesis
- Added _retry_image_capture() + _retry_pending flag: if all 3 attempts fail,
  schedules a 500ms retry (handles Snipping Tool delayed rendering)
- Added INFO-level logging for every capture success/failure so crash.log is useful
- Attempt order: A) Win32 registered PNG/JFIF -> B) Qt image -> C) PIL ImageGrab

release.yml:
- Added --hidden-import for PIL._imaging / PIL.Image / PIL.ImageGrab / PIL.*Plugin
  so PyInstaller one-file build never silently fails Pillow imports

tests (added):
- test_clipboard_diag.py: 11 tests (format IDs, 64-bit handles, synthetic PNG)
- test_monitor_integration.py: 8 tests (PNG-only, CF_DIB, PNG+CF_DIB via real monitor)
- test_retry_mechanism.py: delayed-render retry test

All 20 tests pass.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add Windows build check for PRs

Adds a lightweight build-windows-check job that runs restore + build
on PRs and non-tag pushes, catching Windows build breaks before merge.
The full build-windows job (with signing, installer, artifacts) still
only runs on version tags.

Required status checks to enable on main branch protection:
- build-linux
- build-windows-check

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add perf smoke test, DB index migration, reduce noisy WS logging

- Add perf/baseline.json with p50/p95 baselines for /healthz and /api/ping
- Add scripts/perf-smoke.js: runs 30 measured requests per endpoint,
  checks absolute 800ms p95 ceiling and 30% tolerance over baseline
- Add npm scripts: perf:smoke and perf:baseline
- Add CI step: Run performance smoke test (continue-on-error: true)
- Add db/migrations/007_add_users_tier_index.sql: idx_users_tier and
  idx_users_email_lower for subscription queries and admin stats
- Wrap [WS] Client connected and sent-type logs behind DEBUG=true guard

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
